seoTitle: Create and Use a Microsoft Loop Page Template
metaDescription: Save time on repeat documentation by using built-in Loop templates or turning your own formatted page into a reusable, shareable template.
intro: Rebuilding the same meeting notes layout from scratch every week wastes time and leads to inconsistent formatting across teams. This guide covers both applying one of Loop's built-in templates and turning your own page into a custom template that others on your team can reuse.

steps.heading: Use a built-in template
steps.body: Create a fresh page, scroll to the bottom of the canvas, and select a layout from the template row.

steps.heading: Apply the template
steps.body: Click through the preview drawer, choose your desired option, and click 'Use this template' to apply the structure.

steps.heading: Or build a custom layout
steps.body: Format a page with your own tables, task lists, and brand layout strings exactly as you want your team to see them.

steps.heading: Save it as a template
steps.body: Click the three dots icon in the top right page options and select 'Turn into Template.'

steps.heading: Name and publish the template
steps.body: Enter a descriptive name, assign a category, and click Save to lock the layout into your shared team gallery.

steps.heading: Test the new template
steps.body: Spawn a brand new page and verify your custom template appears ready for one-click deployment.

faq.question: Can I edit a template after I've already saved it?
faq.answer: Yes, you can update the source page and re-save it as a template, though existing pages already created from the older version won't retroactively change.

faq.question: Are custom templates visible to my whole team?
faq.answer: Custom templates are saved to your team's shared gallery, making them available to anyone with access to that workspace.

faq.question: Do built-in templates cover common use cases like meeting notes?
faq.answer: Yes, Microsoft provides ready-made layout models for common formats like meeting trackers, project hubs, and FAQ logs.

faq.question: What happens to existing pages if I update a shared template?
faq.answer: Updating a template shouldn't retroactively alter pages already created from it, since each page becomes an independent copy once generated.
